title: Document Harpy threat model
body:
## Summary
Write a short threat model for Harpy assuming deployment on a LAN or small VPS with eventual real users.

## Threats to cover
- Tampered block/chain → cumulative work + signed blocks
- DoS via `POST /new-block` → rate limits, payload caps, optional API key
- Disk corruption → atomic writes + checksum
- Unauthorized writes → signed transactions or API token
- Double-spend → UTXO/account state
- Eclipse/fork attacks → P2P diversity + most-work rule

## Deliverable
`docs/threat-model.md` with actors, assets, trust boundaries, and phase mapping.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Lists assumptions and out-of-scope items
- [ ] Maps threats to roadmap phases
- [ ] States Harpy is educational, not production

title: Add cumulative work scoring for fork choice
body:
## Summary
Replace block-count-only fork choice with cumulative proof-of-work scoring.

## Implementation
- Add `Chain#cumulative_work` (e.g. `sum(2**difficulty)` per block)
- Update `replace_if_longer_valid!` to compare work, not just height
- Expose work score on `GET /validate`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Longer but lower-work chain is rejected
- [ ] Higher-work chain wins even if shorter
- [ ] `/validate` returns `work` field
- [ ] `crystal spec` covers fork-by-work

## Depends on
PR #1 baseline validation

title: Enforce monotonic block timestamps
body:
## Summary
Reject blocks whose timestamp is before their parent's timestamp.

## Implementation
- Add check in `Block#valid_against?`
- Use UTC consistently; document timestamp format

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `timestamp < parent.timestamp` fails validation
- [ ] Spec covers valid and invalid cases
- [ ] Genesis exempt from parent check

title: Cap block data payload size
body:
## Summary
Reject oversize block data before mining to prevent memory/DoS abuse.

## Implementation
- Define `MAX_BLOCK_DATA_BYTES` (e.g. 64 KB)
- Validate in HTTP handler before `Miner.mine_next`
- Validate in `Chain#append!` for defense in depth

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] POST with data > cap returns 400
- [ ] Oversize blocks rejected without mining
- [ ] Spec asserts limit

title: Add canonical hash test vectors for crystal-blockchain compatibility
body:
## Summary
Ensure Harpy hash algorithm remains verifiable against upstream tutorial blocks.

## Implementation
- Add fixture blocks from [crystal-blockchain](https://github.com/bradford-hamilton/crystal-blockchain)
- Spec asserts `computed_hash` matches expected values
- Document multiline hash input format

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] At least genesis + one mined block vector
- [ ] Canonical format documented in spec or AGENTS.md

title: Property tests for tamper detection and weak forks
body:
## Summary
Add specs proving random tampering invalidates chains and weak forks lose.

## Implementation
- Tamper hash, nonce, index, prev_hash → `valid?` false
- Competing chains: lower-work fork rejected

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Multiple tamper scenarios in `spec/`
- [ ] Weak fork rejection tested with cumulative work

title: Integration test: most-work chain wins fork
body:
## Summary
End-to-end spec: two competing valid chains; node selects most cumulative work.

## Implementation
- Build chains A and B sharing a common prefix
- Fork with different mining effort
- Assert fork selector picks correct tip

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Integration spec in `spec/`
- [ ] Behavior documented in AGENTS.md

title: Rate limit POST /new-block
body:
## Summary
Prevent CPU exhaustion from unlimited mining requests.

## Implementation
- Per-IP token bucket via Kemal middleware
- Configurable limits via env (e.g. `HARPY_RATE_LIMIT`)
- Return 429 with `Retry-After` when exceeded

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Flood POST returns 429
- [ ] Legitimate traffic still works under limit
- [ ] Spec or integration test for rate limit behavior

title: Enforce JSON request body size limit
body:
## Summary
Reject oversized HTTP bodies before parsing/mining.

## Implementation
- Set max body size in Kemal config or middleware
- Return 413/400 with clear error

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Oversize body rejected before mining starts
- [ ] Limit documented in README/AGENTS.md

title: Optional async mining endpoint (202 + job id)
body:
## Summary
Separate mine from append so CPU abuse is bounded and clients can poll status.

## Implementation
- `POST /mine` returns 202 + job id
- `GET /mine/:id` returns status/result
- Bounded job queue with TTL

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Mining no longer blocks HTTP thread indefinitely (or document sync-only mode)
- [ ] Queue cap prevents unbounded memory growth
- [ ] Documented API in AGENTS.md

title: Bind server to 127.0.0.1 by default
body:
## Summary
Safe default: local-only binding unless explicitly configured for public exposure.

## Implementation
- Default Kemal bind `127.0.0.1`
- `HARPY_HOST=0.0.0.0` to expose publicly
- README warning when binding publicly

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Default bind is loopback
- [ ] Public bind requires explicit env var
- [ ] Documented in README

title: Environment-based configuration
body:
## Summary
Centralize runtime config via environment variables.

## Variables
- `HARPY_API_KEY` — optional auth for write endpoints
- `HARPY_DATA_DIR` — chain storage path
- `HARPY_DIFFICULTY` — PoW difficulty override
- `HARPY_HOST` / `HARPY_PORT`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All vars documented with defaults
- [ ] Server reads env on boot
- [ ] Specs cover config parsing where practical

title: Structured logging for chain events
body:
## Summary
Log block accepted/rejected and validation failures without leaking secrets.

## Implementation
- Structured log lines (JSON or key=value)
- Events: block_accepted, block_rejected, chain_invalid, storage_error
- Never log API keys or private material

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Validation failures produce actionable logs
- [ ] No secrets in log output
- [ ] Documented log fields

title: Add GET /health endpoint
body:
## Summary
Operational health check for deployments and monitoring.

## Response
- `valid`: chain validation status
- `height`: block count
- `last_save_at`: timestamp of last successful persistence
- `work`: cumulative work (when available)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Returns 200 when chain valid
- [ ] Returns 503 when chain invalid
- [ ] Documented in AGENTS.md API table

title: Atomic chain file writes
body:
## Summary
Prevent corrupted chain files from partial writes.

## Implementation
- Write to temp file in same directory
- `fsync` then atomic rename over `data/chain.json`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Kill during write → restart loads previous valid chain or fails safe
- [ ] Spec simulates interrupted write (temp file left behind handled)

title: Add SHA-256 checksum envelope for chain persistence
body:
## Summary
Detect on-disk tampering and corruption via checksum.

## Implementation
- Envelope: `{version, checksum, blocks}`
- Checksum over canonical JSON of blocks array
- Reject load on mismatch with clear `StorageError`

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Tampered file rejected at boot
- [ ] Round-trip spec preserves checksum
- [ ] Version field present for migrations

title: Chain storage schema versioning and migrations
body:
## Summary
Support evolving on-disk format without silent corruption.

## Implementation
- `version` field in storage envelope
- Migration path v1 → v2 when schema changes
- Fail with actionable error on unknown version

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Version bump documented
- [ ] At least one migration test or stub
- [ ] Unknown version does not silently load

title: Spike embedded KV backend (RocksDB/LMDB) behind Storage interface
body:
## Summary
Evaluate real embedded DB for scale; keep JSON fallback on Windows preview.

## Implementation
- Abstract `Harpy::Storage` backend trait/interface
- Spike Crystal shard for RocksDB or LMDB
- Document Windows/Crystal preview limitations

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] JSON backend still works
- [ ] Spike doc with go/no-go recommendation
- [ ] No regression in existing storage specs

title: CLI: export-chain and verify-chain commands
body:
## Summary
Backup and offline validation tooling for operators.

## Implementation
- `shards build` binary or `crystal run` subcommands
- `harpy export-chain` → stdout or file
- `harpy verify-chain <file>` → exit 0/1

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Export produces validatable JSON
- [ ] Verify detects invalid/tampered chains
- [ ] Documented in README

title: Design doc: UTXO state model
body:
## Summary
**Design gate** — pick and document state model before P2P or reorgs.

## Decision
Research recommends UTXO for learning double-spend prevention.

## Deliverable
- UTXO set schema
- Coinbase rules and block reward
- Maturity depth (e.g. 100 blocks for reorg safety)
- Undo data requirements for reorgs

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Design reviewed before implementation issues start
- [ ] Linked from AGENTS.md
- [ ] Explicitly blocks Phase 5 until approved

title: Implement signed Transaction struct
body:
## Summary
Replace free-form `data` string with structured, signed transactions.

## Fields
- `from`, `to`, `amount`, `fee`, `signature`, inputs/outputs (UTXO)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Transaction JSON serializable
- [ ] Invalid signature rejected
- [ ] Block body carries tx list not arbitrary string

title: Ed25519 keypair signing and verification
body:
## Summary
Cryptographic ownership of transactions.

## Implementation
- Crystal `crypto` stdlib or shard for Ed25519
- Sign tx hash; verify on mempool and block acceptance
- Document key format for CLI/wallet (future)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Valid signature passes; invalid fails
- [ ] Spec covers tampered tx body
- [ ] No private keys logged

title: Mempool with transaction validation
body:
## Summary
Hold pending transactions; validate before mining.

## Implementation
- `Harpy::Mempool` with add/remove/evict
- Validate balance, signature, double-spend against UTXO set
- Reject invalid txs at API boundary

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Double-spend in mempool rejected
- [ ] Insufficient balance rejected
- [ ] Miner pulls from mempool, not raw POST data

title: Miner includes mempool transactions in blocks
body:
## Summary
Blocks carry validated transactions from mempool.

## Implementation
- Select txs up to block size limit
- Optional merkle root over tx ids
- Coinbase tx for block reward

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Mined block contains mempool txs
- [ ] Empty mempool still mines coinbase-only block
- [ ] Spec covers multi-tx block

title: Block reward and fee sink
body:
## Summary
Minimal tokenomics: coinbase reward + fees to miner.

## Implementation
- Fixed block reward constant
- Fees sum from included txs
- UTXO set updated on block apply

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Coinbase creates new UTXO for miner
- [ ] Fees attributed correctly
- [ ] Spec verifies supply conservation

title: Tests: double-spend, insufficient balance, bad signature
body:
## Summary
Core state security test suite.

## Scenarios
- Spend same UTXO twice (mempool + blocks)
- Spend more than balance
- Wrong signature / wrong key

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] All scenarios fail closed
- [ ] `crystal spec` green
- [ ] Tests documented as security invariants

title: P2P gossip transport between nodes
body:
## Summary
Multi-node block propagation.

## Implementation
- Evaluate lightweight TCP vs Crystal libp2p shard
- Message types: `inv`, `block`, `get_blocks`
- Configurable peer list / bootstrap nodes

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Two nodes sync blocks over network
- [ ] Documented run instructions for multi-node local test
- [ ] Depends on Phase 4 UTXO design complete

title: Orphan block pool for out-of-order arrivals
body:
## Summary
Buffer blocks received before their parent is known.

## Implementation
- Orphan pool keyed by prev_hash
- Promote to chain when parent arrives
- TTL/size cap on orphan pool

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Block 105 before 104 is retained, not dropped
- [ ] Orphan pool bounded
- [ ] Spec or integration test

title: Most-cumulative-work fork choice over network
body:
## Summary
Network-wide fork resolution using work score.

## Implementation
- Compare incoming chains by cumulative work
- Replace local tip when heavier valid chain received
- Integrate with orphan handling

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Heavier fork becomes tip
- [ ] Lighter fork rejected
- [ ] Uses Phase 1 cumulative work

title: Reorg handler with per-block undo data
body:
## Summary
Safely roll back and apply forks without corrupting UTXO state.

## Implementation
- Store undo data per block (spent/created UTXOs)
- On reorg: unwind blocks, restore UTXO set, apply new chain
- Budget ~350+ LOC per research reference

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Reorg restores exact prior UTXO set
- [ ] No funds created or destroyed incorrectly
- [ ] Unit tests for unwind/rewind

title: Peer limits, handshake, and ban misbehaving peers
body:
## Summary
Basic network abuse resistance.

## Implementation
- Max connections per peer/IP
- Handshake with chain id / genesis hash
- Ban peers sending invalid blocks repeatedly

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Invalid block flood does not stall node
- [ ] Wrong genesis peer rejected
- [ ] Ban list evicts bad actors

title: Reject blocks failing full validation and state transition
body:
## Summary
Defense in depth at network boundary.

## Implementation
- Full block + PoW + tx + UTXO transition validation before accept
- Never persist or relay invalid blocks

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Invalid block from peer not appended
- [ ] Invalid block not relayed
- [ ] Logged with peer id

title: Integration test: 3 nodes, reorg, UTXO consistency
body:
## Summary
**Critical** multi-node test from research §4.2.

## Scenario
1. Spin up 3 nodes
2. Mine on competing forks
3. Trigger reorg
4. Assert all nodes converge; UTXO sets match

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Automated integration test (may use subprocesses)
- [ ] Documented in README as release gate
- [ ] Catches reorg accounting bugs

title: Difficulty retargeting from observed block times
body:
## Summary
Adjust PoW difficulty every N blocks to target block interval.

## Implementation
- Track timestamps of last retarget window
- Adjust difficulty up/down with bounds
- Store difficulty in block header (already present)

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Fast blocks → difficulty increases
- [ ] Slow blocks → difficulty decreases
- [ ] Spec with synthetic timestamps

title: Document probabilistic vs strong finality
body:
## Summary
Explain Harpy's finality model for educational clarity.

## Content
- PoW probabilistic finality
- How depth reduces reorg risk
- Why coinbase maturity matters

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Doc in `docs/` or AGENTS.md
- [ ] Links to research §3 finality discussion

title: Minimum transaction fee anti-spam
body:
## Summary
Prevent mempool flooding once transactions exist.

## Implementation
- `MIN_TX_FEE` constant or dynamic floor
- Reject below-minimum fee txs

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Zero-fee spam rejected (unless coinbase)
- [ ] Spec covers boundary fee

title: Coinbase maturity before spendable
body:
## Summary
Coinbase outputs unspendable until N confirmations (reorg safety).

## Implementation
- Track coinbase maturity depth (e.g. 100 blocks)
- UTXO spend check includes maturity

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Immature coinbase cannot be spent
- [ ] Mature coinbase can be spent
- [ ] Spec covers edge at maturity boundary

title: Threat model for smart contract layer
body:
## Summary
OWASP/a16z-style threat model before any VM work.

## Deliverable
- Attack surfaces: reentrancy, access control, gas griefing
- Explicit out-of-scope for educational VM

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Reviewed before VM implementation
- [ ] Linked from Phase 7 issues

title: Minimal stack VM with gas metering
body:
## Summary
Tiny stack-based VM to teach EVM-style execution limits.

## Implementation
- Opcode set: arithmetic, storage, halt
- Gas cost per opcode; tx gas limit
- Halt on out-of-gas

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Infinite loop tx runs out of gas
- [ ] Storage writes cost more than arithmetic
- [ ] Spec covers gas exhaustion

title: Fuzz and property tests for VM
body:
## Summary
Stress-test VM and state transitions.

## Implementation
- Random opcode sequences within gas limit
- Property: total gas used <= limit
- Property: state rollback on failure

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Fuzz/property suite in CI
- [ ] Document how to run locally

title: No upgradeable contracts without timelock story
body:
## Summary
If upgrade path exists, document governance + timelock requirements.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Either no upgrades, or timelock + multisig documented
- [ ] OWASP proxy/upgrade category addressed

title: External audit checklist before real value
body:
## Summary
Pre-mainnet checklist (educational reference).

## References
- [OWASP SCS](https://scs.owasp.org/)
- [a16z crypto checklist](https://a16zcrypto.com/posts/article/smart-contract-security-checklist-web3-development/)
- Solodit / SCSFG

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] `docs/audit-checklist.md` adapted for Harpy scope
- [ ] Explicitly states Harpy is not production-ready
